UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores user-specific data required for lunar calculations and app personalization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

birthDate.description: The user's birth date, used to calculate lunar age and the number of full moons visible since birth.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

NewsArticleSummary.title: NewsArticleSummary
NewsArticleSummary.description: Stores AI-generated summaries of environmental and nature news articles, allowing users to review curated content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NewsArticleSummary entity.

title.description: The title or headline of the summarized news article.

summaryText.description: The AI-generated textual summary of the news article.

originalUrl.description: The URL of the original news article from which the summary was generated.

publishedDate.description: The date when the original news article was published.

retrievedDate.description: The date and time when the news summary was retrieved or generated by the app.

StickyNote.title: StickyNote
StickyNote.description: Stores user-created short notes, memos, or reminders, providing a personal persistent information tracking feature.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StickyNote entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that owns this sticky note. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N StickyNote)

title.description: An optional short title or subject for the sticky note.

content.description: The main text content of the sticky note.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the sticky note was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the sticky note was last updated.

isArchived.description: A boolean flag indicating whether the sticky note has been archived (true) or is currently active (false).

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`{userId}`) matches the Firebase Authentication UID. This ensures path-based ownership, making user profile data private and securely accessible only by the authenticated user. No authorization denormalization is required beyond the document ID matching the UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated summaries of environmental and nature news articles. This collection is intended for public read access by all users. Write operations are restricted to backend services. All documents share a homogeneous security posture (publicly readable).

params.name: newsArticleId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific news article summary.

definition.description: Stores user-created sticky notes, memos, and reminders. This is a subcollection under a specific user's profile, ensuring private access. The 'userId' field within the StickyNote document is denormalized from the parent path for authorization independence, allowing security rules to verify ownership via 'request.auth.uid == userId' without requiring a 'get()' call.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this sticky note, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: stickyNoteId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific sticky note.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for LunarEchoes is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling, ensuring robust, debuggable security rules and efficient data access. 

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **User Profile (`/users/{userId}`):** User profiles are stored in a top-level collection where the document ID (`{userId}`) directly corresponds to the authenticated user's UID (`request.auth.uid`). This inherently ensures authorization independence as access can be granted by simply checking `request.auth.uid == userId` without needing any `get()` operations. Each user directly owns their profile, and there's no hierarchical dependency for authorization. 
2.  **Sticky Notes (`/users/{userId}/sticky_notes/{stickyNoteId}`):** Sticky notes are structured as a subcollection under the user's profile. This path structure places the `userId` directly in the path (`/users/{userId}/...`), allowing security rules to verify `request.auth.uid == userId`. Crucially, the `StickyNote` entity also contains a `userId` field. This denormalization (the `userId` in the document matches the `userId` in the path) provides redundant verification and makes authorization explicit and atomic, eliminating any need for `get()` calls to a parent document for authorization. A user can create, read, update, or delete their own sticky notes without checking permissions on the parent user profile document.

**Querying and Access Patterns (QAPs):**
1.  **User Profile:** Access to a specific user profile (`/users/{userId}`) is straightforward. Since `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`, only the authenticated user can `get` their own profile. List operations for this collection are implicitly restricted by design, as users can only query their own specific profile document.
2.  **News Article Summaries (`/news_articles/{newsArticleId}`):** This collection is designed for public read access, implying that all documents within it share the same security posture. This structural segregation allows for simple rules: anyone can `read` all documents. `list` queries are fully supported for all users without any filtering concerns in security rules, as all data is intended to be public. Only administrative backend processes would have `create`, `update`, `delete` permissions.
3.  **Sticky Notes:** The hierarchical path `/users/{userId}/sticky_notes/{stickyNoteId}` ensures that `list` queries for a user's sticky notes (`/users/{userId}/sticky_notes`) are secure. Rules can enforce that `request.auth.uid == userId` for any `list` operation, guaranteeing that a user can only query their own notes. This adheres to the QAP principle by making the authorization intent explicit in the path and document data, allowing efficient and secure listing of user-owned data.

**Overall Strategy:**
*   **Structural Segregation** is maintained by separating user-private data (`/users/{userId}` and its subcollections) from globally readable data (`/news_articles`).
*   **Access Modeling** standardizes on Path-Based Ownership for user-specific data, making rules concise and easy to audit.
*   **Predictable Schema** and descriptive wildcards (`{userId}`, `{newsArticleId}`, `{stickyNoteId}`) enhance debuggability and maintainability.
